Empires & Puzzles Astrid Review: The Resilient Bard of Resurrection

Astrid isn't just a bard; she's a one-woman fortress. With stats that defied gravity in 2024 and still hold up in 2026, plus a passive that shuts down the entire Opera family, she's the revivalist you didn't know you needed. We break down why this 'Average' speed hero is anything but average.
Astrid Review: The Heavy Metal Healer Who Stops The Dance
Astrid: The "Red Button" Reviver Who Silences The Dance Floor
Meet Astrid, the jovial, drum-beating powerhouse from the
event (Bard Family). Released back in late 2024, she arrived with a stat spread so defensively weighted it looked like a typo. She is a
who functions as a Healer, Reviver, and massive defensive support.

While many heroes from her era have faded into obscurity, Astrid remains relevant for two reasons: she is incredibly hard to kill, and she possesses a passive ability that acts as a hard counter to some of the most annoying meta mechanics in the game (looking at you, Opera dancers). She isn't here to deal damage; she's here to make sure your team simply refuses to die.

Astrid breaks the mold of the brooding warrior or ethereal elf. She is depicted as a joyous, fuller-figured bard with vibrant orange hair, enthusiastically beating a large drum. It's a refreshing, grounded design that screams "heart of the party" rather than "edgy assassin." The art perfectly captures her role: she's the sturdy backbone of the band, keeping the rhythm going when everyone else has fallen. Plus, the little blue bird flying next to her adds a nice touch of whimsy amidst the chaos of battle.
When Astrid was released, her defensive stats were considered "game-breaking." Now, in 2026, they are simply "excellent."
This is incredibly low by modern standards, where attackers push 1350+. However, Astrid never needs to attack, so this "dump stat" is perfectly fine.
Still very competitive. While modern tanks push 1300+, Astrid's natural bulk allows her to survive sniper hits that would obliterate other older supports.
This is her crown jewel. Even against the latest 2026 power creep, 2250+ HP is top-tier. She was future-proofed with health, keeping her survivability rating high.
Her stats have aged like fine wine. She sacrifices offense entirely to remain one of the tankiest healers in the game.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
This is where Astrid truly shines and earns her spot in the 2026 meta.

bonus is excellent for roster diversity. Gaining +5% mana generation just for being the only Bard is a free troop upgrade for your whole team. It allows "Slow" heroes to charge in 11 tiles or "Average" heroes in 9 tiles much easier.

Upon release, Astrid was feared for her "Red Button" potential and massive stats. She was a staple in Rush Wars and a frequent sight on defense wings.
While her Attack stat is laughable by modern standards, her bulk has kept her relevant.
She is no longer the "must-have" tank she might have been, but she is a top-tier support for

tournaments and wars. Her passive makes her an eternal counter-pick against Dance/Ballad metas. She is not a primary healer for aggressive PvP due to average speed, but she is a safety net that refuses to break.
Astrid is a wall. Build her like one.

If you pulled Astrid, you have your tank/healer for the foreseeable future. Put her in the center of your defense until you reach Diamond arena. Focus on keeping her alive; she will save your team from mistakes.
She is a luxury tool. Don't strip emblems from your primary war tank for her, but keep her ready for
need the second Alpha Aether unless you use her in your main war defense; standard LB1 is sufficient for her stats to hold up.
The community's reaction to Astrid was mixed but passionate.
"I think people overrate revivers in general... but this card almost gives revive 'for free'... The stats are ridiculous."
Another famously called her passive a
lamenting that strategy was turning into plug-and-play.
Interestingly, many players realized her true value wasn't just the revive, but the passive:
"She is a great counter to Von Rothbart... since Astrid was the first new bard she is the most common."

Astrid is the heavy metal drummer who refuses to let the song end. While she lacks the offensive pressure of modern "do-it-all" heroes, her ability to absorb punishment and bring back the dead ensures she remains a thorn in the side of attackers. She shuts down the dancers, protects the weak, and occasionally, with that 1 damage reduction, performs a miracle.
She might not be the most exciting hero to play
, but she is absolutely one of the most annoying to play
. And in Empires & Puzzles, being annoying is a top-tier trait.
A timeless defensive anchor. Keep her, level her, and let the drum solo continue.
A comprehensive review and strategy guide for Astrid, the Holy 5-star Bard hero. Discover why her massive defense and anti-Opera passives make her a timeless support hero.
